g.text: US-001: Zero Critical Violations in CI
As a developer, I need the CI/CD pipeline to automatically block
deployments with accessibility violations so that we never ship
As a user with low vision, I need all text to meet the 7:1 contrast
ratio so that I can read content without strain or assistive tools.
Defines AAA vs AA (7:1 vs 4.5:1 contrast ratio)
As a user who cannot use a mouse, I need to navigate and operate
all functionality using only a keyboard so that I have equal access.
Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrow keys per ARIA patterns
As a user with motor impairments, I need all interactive elements to
be at least 44x44 pixels so that I can accurately tap or click them.
As a blind user relying on a screen reader, I need all content to be
announced correctly so I can understand and interact with the app.
As a user who needs extra time to read or complete tasks, I need no
time-based restrictions so that I can work at my own pace.
As a user with cognitive disabilities, I need content written at a lower
secondary education reading level so that I can understand it.
As a developer, I need real-time accessibility feedback during
development so I can fix issues immediately rather than in CI.
As a project stakeholder, I need a dashboard showing accessibility
compliance status to monitor progress and identify problem areas.
100% of pages pass WCAG 2.1 AAA automated tests (zero critical violations)
All text meets 7:1 contrast ratio (verified by automated testing)
All interactive elements are at least 44x44 CSS pixels
Application is fully navigable using keyboard only (verified by manual audit)
Screen reader announces all content correctly (verified with NVDA/VoiceOver)
No time-based restrictions exist anywhere in the application
Content scores at grade 9 or below on Flesch-Kincaid readability
CI pipeline catches 95%+ of accessibility regressions before merge
Real-time dev feedback latency is under 2 seconds after file save
Dashboard displays historical data for at least 90 days
